title: Ex-Ditector Public Attack-Surface Self-Scan Input
description: Audits a domain YOU OWN for publicly-discoverable subdomains (certificate-transparency logs) and a leaked registrant email (public WHOIS/RDAP). Subdomain discovery is a dual-use attack-surface-management technique, so the scope enum intentionally contains ONLY self and public_figure; consented/brand/safety_evidence cannot run this actor. You must explicitly assert that you own or are authorized to audit the domain.

scope_type.title: Scope type
scope_type.description: Attack-surface discovery is permitted ONLY for your own domain (self) or a genuine public figure's publicly-operated domain. Other compliant scopes are intentionally absent.

domain.title: Apex domain you own
domain.description: The apex domain to audit, e.g. "example.com". Only hosts that belong to THIS domain are inventoried; certificate SANs for other domains are discarded. Do not enter a domain you do not own or are not authorized to audit.

i_own_this_domain.title: I confirm I own or am authorized to audit this domain
i_own_this_domain.description: Required. This actor performs a SELF attack-surface inventory; it must not be pointed at infrastructure you do not control. The run is refused unless this is checked.

check_whois.title: Check public WHOIS/RDAP for a leaked registrant email
check_whois.description: If true, query the PUBLIC RDAP record for the domain to detect a registrant email exposed in WHOIS (so you can enable registrar privacy). The email is converted to a k-anonymity prefix + masked display; the full address is never stored.

max_subdomains.title: Max subdomains to report
max_subdomains.description: Cap on how many discovered subdomains to emit, to keep the inventory readable. Discovery itself is bounded by the CT log response.

subject_label.title: Subject label
subject_label.description: Human label for the subject (e.g. your name or org). Used only for reporting.
